items.title: Drone footage reveals extent of seal deaths from bird flu on Australian island

children.content: Research has revealed the devastating impact of bird flu on a remote Australian territory where more than 13,000 baby seals are now believed to have been killed by the virus.

items.title: Greens leader apologises for comparing premier to Russian dictator

children.content: The leader of the Tasmanian Greens has apologised for likening Premier Jeremy Rockliff to Joseph Stalin, the infamous Soviet dictator.

image.altText: Black and while photo of Russian Communist leader, Joseph Stalin, in uniform minus hat.

items.title: AI factory 'brings into doubt' Marinus Link future

children.content: Tasmania's cool climate and access to renewable energy has made it an attractive place for AI data centre investment, but energy industry voices are warning that the business case for the Marinus Link project appears increasingly unviable if power-hungry players such as AI data centres continue to expand across the state.

image.altText: Man stands in front of a large drum of cable.

items.title: The pros and cons of retiring overseas with your pension

children.content: With some foreign countries offering cheaper living expenses, it's no surprise that moving overseas to retire is increasingly attractive to some older Australians. But before you up stumps and book that flight, read this.

Asian city street at nighttime with lights and motorbikes .

items.title: Sell-out shows, cult status line-up: How Dark Mofo catapults artists

children.content: Her headline show at Dark Mofo sold out in just over an hour, something Tasmanian Indigenous singer Miss Kaninna says couldn't have happened without the winter festival's ability to attract "massive, massive" music industry names.

image.altText: Miss Kaninna sits in an armchair in a room with red lighting.

items.title: Devils coaching choice both a blessing and a curse

articles.title: The 'mental toughness' that created an Aussie World Cup 'superstar'

children.content: At just 22, Patrick Beach stunned the world in his FIFA World Cup debut and third international start with the Socceroos against Türkiye last weekend.

image.altText: A composite of Beach in Socceroos uniform pumping his fist in celebration next to him as a child playing soccer.

articles.title: Messi calls for 'humanity' from media as father receives medical treatment

children.content: Argentina superstar Lionel Messi and his family ask the media for "humanity" amid rumours about his father Jorge Messi's health.

items.title: Oil flows through strait but Lebanon strikes raise peace doubts

children.content: Some 12.5 million barrels of crude oil have sailed through the Strait of Hormuz overnight, US Vice President JD Vance says, but in Lebanon, Israel launched fresh air strikes, raising doubts over peace in the region.

image.altText: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz near the beach of Bandar Abbas.

items.title: Democrats chewing nails as Trump targets controversial Senate pick

children.content: A Democrat is accused of being a Republican in disguise; a Republican is accused of being a Democrat plant. It's primaries season in the US, and suspicions of dirty pool are rife.

items.title: EV public charger shortage? The data shows drivers rarely queue to charge

children.content: It's often claimed Australia has an EV public charger shortage, but analysis of exclusive data shows most fast charger sites are not congested.

items.title: Man arrested after boy critically injured in crocodile enclosure at UK zoo

children.content: British police say they have arrested a man on suspicion of attempted murder after being called to a popular zoo north of London.

items.title: Ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood is dead

children.content: The tree is said to have sheltered Robin Hood, the legendary 13th-century bandit who stole from the rich and gave to the poor and took refuge in the Nottingham forest.

children.content: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has asked a court to order the company behind a film involving Brittany Higgins to hand over raw footage.

items.title: Would you live in a 1930s pump station? This couple made one their home

children.content: From former post offices to pumping stations, there are buildings with commercial histories all around Australia. Could repurposing them into homes help with the housing supply?

children.content: The multi-billion-dollar franchise has overcome rewrites, accidental deletions and generations of changing tastes to bring Toy Story 5 to cinemas in 2026.

items.title: Migration dips as Coalition accuses government of failing to meet targets

children.content: Net overseas migration remained above 300,000 last year, fuelling opposition claims Labor has locked in an unsustainable new normal despite the number of arrivals continuing to fall.

items.title: Hegseth blasts NATO members, announces review of US forces in Europe

items.title: Fire authorities failed to meet targets ahead of 2024 Grampians blazes

children.content: An auditor-general's report finds fire authorities missed planned fuel reduction targets before two disastrous fire seasons and warns properties could be at risk if strategic fuel breaks are not put in place.

items.title: Fashion boss was 'scary and violent' in alleged rape, trial hears

children.content: A woman says she was scared to leave the Byron Bay holiday home of a Melbourne man, who she alleges forced her to engage in 11 non-consensual sex acts over a four hour-period.

items.title: Coroner suggests racism marred investigation of Indigenous teen's death

children.content: The mysterious death of Mark Haines, whose body was discovered on railway tracks in northern NSW in 1988, would have been treated differently "if it was a white boy", according to the coroner.

image.altText: A composite image of Mark Haines with railway tracks.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands's $12m settlement won't deter Jackie O's legal battle

children.content: A legal battle between Jackie "O" Henderson and ARN Media appears still headed for an October trial after her former co-host Kyle Sandilands settled his own court case against the company.

children.content: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

items.title: NT government reveals scale of bungled smart meter rollout

children.content: The Northern Territory's main energy retailer, Jacana, reveals about 5,000 customers — thousands more than previously thought — have been caught up in a smart meter bungle leading to long delays in their billing.

items.title: Sarah Game won't rule out introducing another bill to restrict late term abortion

children.content: Family First upper house MLC Sarah Game has not ruled out introducing another bill restricting late-term abortion access, after her proposed legislation to restrict abortion after 25 weeks failed in the lower house last night.
